articles.title: NSE IPO: 10 key risks investors should know from the DRHP
articles.summary: NSE filed its Draft Red Herring Prospectus for a proposed ₹30,000 crore IPO, expected to be the largest in India. LIC holds a 10.72% stake. The offering consists of 14.89 crore equity shares, with proceeds going to existing shareholders.
articles.label: NEUTRAL

articles.title: Nocil share price jump 20% on anti-dumping boost. Do you own?
articles.summary: Nocil share price opened at ₹165.09 per share today, as compared to previous close of ₹159.02 on Friday last week.
articles.label: BULLISH

articles.title: Bajaj Auto buyback record date nears. Should you participate in ₹5,633 crore share repurchase deal?
articles.summary: In May, Bajaj Auto's board approved a share buyback proposal of up to 46.94 lakh equity shares with a face value of ₹10 each, representing 1.68% of the company's paid-up equity capital
articles.label: BULLISH

articles.title: Why Amber Enterprises’ Oppo deal comes with caveats
articles.summary: Amber Enterprises is entering smartphone manufacturing through Oppo, betting on localization and scale. But weak margins, slowing customer volumes and fierce competition pose challenges.
articles.label: BEARISH

articles.title: Riyaasat Lifestyle IPO Day 3: SME IPO subscribed 10% so far on last day. Check GMP, price band, other key details
articles.summary: Riyaasat Lifestyle IPO price band is set at ₹102 to ₹108 per share, and the IPO lot size is 1,200 shares. At the upper-end of the price band, the company plans to raise ₹30.77 crore from the book-building issue, which is entirely a fresh issue of 28.48 lakh equity shares.
articles.label: BULLISH

articles.title: Liotech Industries IPO: Focus shifts to allotment date. Latest GMP, step-by-step guide to check status
articles.summary: Liotech Industries IPO share allotment concludes on 22 June. Investors can check their status via Kfin Technologies. The IPO's GMP is ₹0, with a subscription status of 1.91x. Refunds for non-allocation start 22 June, while allocated shares will be credited by 23 June.
articles.label: BULLISH

articles.title: Clay Craft IPO share allotment likely today; GMP, how to check status in 4 steps
articles.summary: Clay Craft IPO share allotment is set to be finalised today, following an impressive subscription rate. Here is how investors can check their allotment status.

articles.summary: One crore equity shares of Blue Cloud Softech Solutions changed hands on BSE as compared to its weekly average trading volumes of 8 lakh and monthly average of 9 lakh shares.

articles.title: Leapfrog Engineering Services IPO allotment date likely today. GMP, steps to check share allotment status of SME IPO

Investors can check Leapfrog Engineering IPO allotment status online through the websites of BSE and the IPO registrar. Integrated Registry Management Services Pvt Ltd is the Leapfrog Engineering Services IPO registrar.

articles.title: Turtlemint IPO Day 2: Issue subscribed 49% so far. Check GMP, subscription status, review. Apply or not?
articles.summary: The Turtlemint IPO, valued at over ₹4,500 crore, opened on 19 June and closes on 23 June, with initial investor interest showing 45% subscription. The issue has attracted major institutional investors and is set to debut on 29 June, amid concerns over profitability and valuation.

articles.title: Defence stock Garden Reach Shipbuilders jumps 5% after being conferred with Navratna status
articles.summary: Garden Reach Shipbuilders & Engineers shares rose nearly 5% following the DPE granting Navratna status due to its strong financial performance. Revenue jumped from ₹1,754 crore to ₹7,002 crore in five years, aiding broader initiatives like Atmanirbhar Bharat.

articles.title: Multibagger stock: Kirloskar Oil Engines share price jumps 20% to 52-week high on winning order for data center infra
articles.summary: Kirloskar Oil Engines share price has gained 43% in one month and has rallied 73% in three months. The stock has jumped 95% on a year-to-date (YTD) basis and has delivered multibagger returns of 183% in one year. Over the past three years, Kirloskar Oil Engines share price has surged 488%.
articles.label: BULLISH

articles.title: Gold, silver prices today: Rates rise on MCX on value buying amid progress in US-Iran peace talks
articles.summary: Gold, silver prices today: Rates rose on the MCX due to value buying after media reports suggested progress in US-Iran peace talks. However, an uptick in the US dollar and 10-year bond yields capped gains.

articles.title: Rupee opens 4 paise lower at 94.36 against US dollar
articles.summary: The Indian rupee opened at 94.36, 4 paise weaker against the US dollar on June 22. Despite a 0.8% gain last week and easing crude oil prices, concerns about prolonged depreciation persist as diplomatic progress between the US and Iran supports market sentiment.
articles.label: BEARISH

articles.summary: US-Iran peace deal: Brent crude rose by as much as 2.2% at the market open, reaching $82.30 per barrel, while West Texas Intermediate climbed above the $78 mark, on Monday.

articles.summary: Mahendra Nahata’s 0.54% holding in Jio Platforms, purchased for ₹48 crore in 2020, is now worth ~ ₹5,800 crore, based on a Motilal Oswal analysis that values the company at $114 billion.

articles.summary: Despite global challenges, Wall Street remains optimistic, with US stocks reaching new highs driven by an AI boom. However, JPMorgan CEO Jamie Dimon expresses caution. Here's what he is not fully buying into the market's optimism.
articles.label: BULLISH

articles.title: A stock trader’s guide to navigating a rare ‘Super El Niño’
articles.summary: Investors are reassessing holdings as climate risks rise with a possible Super El Niño impacting agriculture and energy sectors. The situation poses inflationary risks and challenges for central banks, complicating the current equity market environment.

articles.title: US-Iran peace deal: How are falling crude oil prices likely to impact petrol, diesel prices?
articles.summary: US-Iran peace deal: Brent crude prices edged higher on Friday but remained on track for a weekly decline of nearly 8%, as a ceasefire agreement between Israel and Hezbollah eased some geopolitical concerns
articles.label: BULLISH

articles.title: The benefits and risks of RBI’s policy on foreign currency NRI deposits
articles.summary: To shore up the rupee, the government is looking at NRIs to make foreign currency deposits in Indian banks. This has implications for India’s external debt management.
articles.label: BEARISH

articles.title: India, Iran look to soon resume talks to get Chabahar Port plan back on track
articles.summary: India and Iran may resume discussions on restoring normal operations at the strategic Chabahar Port and reviving plans to expand the facility, as geopolitical conditions in the region show signs of stabilising, according to two people aware of the matter.
articles.label: BULLISH

articles.title: Young Punjab kids ‘rich for 4 yrs after selling their lands… then broke’: World Bank's Ajay Banga on urban agri economy
articles.summary: World Bank President Ajay Banga pointed out challenges in urban and agricultural economies, particularly in Punjab, where youth selling farmland quickly spend their earnings, leading to financial instability.
articles.label: NEUTRAL

articles.title: Views, virality and CJP protest: How India's creator economy found a new stage at Jantar Mantar
articles.summary: A May 2025 report, ‘From Content to Commerce: Mapping India’s Creator Economy’ by BCG, said that with over 2–2.5 million monetised content creators influencing more than $350–400 billion in consumer spending, the ecosystem is projected to drive $1 trillion+ in creator-influenced consumption by 2030.
articles.label: BEARISH

articles.title: The week in charts: State debt concerns, WPI inflation, NSE’s IPO debut
articles.summary: In this weekly Plain Facts compilation, we present to you data-based insights, with easy-to-read charts, to help you delve deeper into the stories reported by Mint in the week gone by.
articles.label: BEARISH

articles.title: Development finance should be used strategically to improve governance, service delivery in Northeast: FM Sitharaman
articles.summary: Finance minister Nirmala Sitharaman said that externally aided project funding to India's Northeast has risen nearly eightfold to ₹76,000 crore since 2014, and urged states to use such finance to strengthen governance and service delivery.
articles.label: BULLISH

articles.title: AI boom shields Asia from West Asia shock, but risks mount for India and region: Moody’s Analytics
articles.summary: Moody's Analytics has said that AI-led exports and investment have helped Asia-Pacific weather the fallout from the West Asia conflict, but higher oil prices, inflation, trade tensions and weather-related disruptions could slow growth in the years ahead.
articles.label: BEARISH

articles.title: Watchful and wary: Inside MPC's unanimous decision to hold rates
articles.summary: At its 3-5 June meeting, the Monetary Policy Committee kept the key repo rate unchanged at 5.25%, while acknowledging that there were considerable risks to its assessments of inflation and growth.
articles.label: BEARISH

articles.title: Oil slides below $80 as US-Iran peace deal eases supply fears
articles.summary: Brent slipped below $80 a barrel as the US and Iran signed a peace framework, easing supply concerns and paving the way for the reopening of the Strait of Hormuz.
articles.label: BULLISH

articles.title: Fuel curbs rollback likely as Iran deal raises hope
articles.summary: The Centre is weighing a rollback of emergency curbs on fuel sales imposed during the West Asia conflict after the US and Iran sued for peace, a move that could ease pressure on farmers, lower inflation and improve prospects for the world's third-largest oil importer.
articles.label: BULLISH

articles.title: India secures steel market access under UK FTA; exporters to avail benefits from 15 July
articles.summary: India has secured quota-based protection for its steel exports under the India-UK Ceta, ensuring market access is preserved even as Britain's safeguard measures take effect from 1 July. The broader trade pact will come into force on 15 July.
articles.label: BULLISH

articles.title: Net direct tax collections rise 14.6% to ₹5.21 trillion as of 17 June
articles.summary: Net direct tax collections rose 14.64% to ₹5.21 trillion as of 17 June, led by a 22.5% surge in corporate tax receipts and a near-45% jump in STT collections.
articles.label: NEUTRAL

articles.title: Centre plans to tap worker cess to fund on-site housing for construction workers
articles.summary: The housing ministry is also evaluating a policy to unlock empty airspace above roads and transit lines, helping developers overcome land shortages in dense urban areas.
articles.label: NEUTRAL

articles.title: Basmati rice exports to West Asia poised for rebound after US, Iran sign peace deal
articles.summary: The reopening the Strait of Hormuz is expected to mitigate shipping bottlenecks, boosting trade to the Gulf, which represents a major market for Indian basmati rice.
articles.label: BULLISH

articles.title: As war pressures ebb, edible oil prices may ease 10% ahead of the festive season
articles.summary: India could see edible oil prices fall by up to 10% in coming months as easing West Asia tensions, lower freight costs and higher palm oil supplies from Indonesia and Malaysia improve availability. However, benefits may take time to reach consumers due to existing inventories and supply contracts.
articles.label: NEUTRAL

articles.title: With Iran-US peace deal in sight, IEA projects oil supply surplus in 2027
articles.summary: Global crude oil supply is set to fall by 3.9 million barrels per day (bpd) to 102.4 million bpd this year amid trade disruptions due to the West Asia war, before rebounding by 8 million bpd next year, the International Energy Agency (IEA) said on Wednesday.
articles.label: BEARISH

articles.title: Crude price fall may not last as nations rush to rebuild reserves
articles.summary: Even with a US-Iran peace deal on the horizon, global crude oil prices are not expected to plummet due to a significant supply shortfall.
articles.label: BEARISH

articles.title: States' liabilities rise to ₹90.5 tn in FY25, many breach fiscal gap limit: CAG
articles.summary: As of 31 March 2025, total liabilities of states stood at ₹90.51 trillion, while outstanding public debt reached ₹75.52 trillion. Public debt has more than tripled over the past decade, rising from ₹23.92 trillion in 2015-16, reflecting growing borrowing requirements across states.
articles.label: BEARISH

articles.title: Mint Explainer | Why May’s record high exports warrant a cautious reading
articles.summary: India’s export growth made a dramatic turnaround in April and May. This may be more of a price-led illusion and less of a sustainable trend.
articles.label: BULLISH

articles.title: Shipping lines stay cautious even after US-Iran peace deal, await security clarity before resuming Gulf operations
articles.summary: The US-Iran peace agreement raises hopes for smoother shipping in the Gulf, yet major lines Maersk and Mitsui O.S.K. remain hesitant. The industry awaits essential security assurances before resuming operations as costs surge due to the ongoing maritime conflict.
articles.label: BULLISH

articles.title: Oil continues to trade subdued; market awaits peace deal details, reopening of Strait of Hormuz
articles.summary: Crude prices hovered near three-month lows after the US and Iran agreed on a peace framework, though uncertainty over the Strait of Hormuz reopening kept markets cautious.
articles.label: BULLISH

articles.title: Government raises export duties on diesel, ATF
articles.summary: The revised rates come into effect from Tuesday. The levy on the export of petrol has, however been kept unchanged at ₹1.5 per litre.
articles.label: NEUTRAL

articles.title: US-Iran truce makes life easier for India's MPC, just a tad
articles.summary: A likely end to the US-Iran war this week is expected to comfort RBI's six-member committee that sets India’s interest rates, easing concerns over oil-led imported inflation that had clouded its outlook.
articles.label: NEUTRAL

articles.title: India’s unemployment rate hits 11-month high as rural joblessness rises in May
articles.summary: Higher unemployment in rural India pushed the overall jobless rate to 5.5% in May, while labour-force participation and employment ratios also weakened.
articles.label: BEARISH

articles.title: India's merchandise exports jump 18% in May; trade deficit widens to $28.21 billion
articles.summary: India's merchandise exports rose to $45.20 billion in May, driven by engineering goods and electronics. Imports increased 20.6% to $73.41 billion, widening the trade deficit to $28.21 billion.
articles.label: BEARISH
